The test script should use this helper method instead of inline expect() assertions. Using the provided verification component improves code reusability and maintainability. The method is defined in the page additions file and should be called after the page additions are merged into PrintHistoryPage.

violations.expected_code:
# In PrintHistoryPage.__init__, add to Elements inner class:
class Elements:
    # Existing elements...
    
    # Billing Cycle Period Card elements
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
    complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
    complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
    complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-tooltip']"
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[data-testid='additional-pages-tooltip']"

self.elements = self.Elements()

# Then access in test script as:
print_history_page.elements.plan_pause_info
print_history_page.elements.billing_cycle_period_card
# etc.
violations.explanation: CRITICAL VIOLATION: The page additions file defines locators as direct Locator properties (self.X = page.locator(...)) instead of using the Elements inner class pattern required by the AURA framework. According to the framework architecture documented in the knowledge base and evidenced by existing page objects in the mapping report, page objects must use an Elements inner class that stores selector STRINGS (not Locator objects), and these are accessed via the .elements. namespace. The current pattern creates Locator objects directly as instance attributes, which violates the framework's Page Object Model architecture. The correct pattern is: (1) Define an Elements inner class with string selectors as class attributes, (2) Instantiate self.elements = self.Elements() in __init__, (3) Access in test scripts via page.locator(page_obj.elements.selector_name) or use the elements directly if the page object provides property methods. This is a CRITICAL violation because it fundamentally breaks the framework's POM pattern and will cause inconsistency with all other page objects in the codebase.

fix_details.description: Restructure all locator definitions to use the Elements inner class pattern with string selectors instead of direct Locator object properties
fix_details.required_structure: Elements inner class with string attributes: class Elements: X = 'selector'; then self.elements = self.Elements()

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Restructure the page additions file to use the Elements inner class pattern. Convert all direct Locator property definitions (self.X = page.locator(...)) to Elements class attributes storing selector strings (X = 'selector'). This is the highest priority fix because it addresses a fundamental framework architecture violation that affects how the page object integrates with the rest of the codebase.

Replace inline expect() assertions with the provided verification methods from page additions. Use print_history_page.verify_complimentary_pages_value() for step 8 and print_history_page.verify_additional_pages_value() for step 14. This improves code reusability and consistency by utilizing the verification components that were specifically created for these checks.
